docs.text: DSA4264 JobReady Report Group Members Name Student Number Alvis Low Yue Han Lam Jian Yi Eugene Lee Tze Ning Ryan Toh Jun Hui Yap Yi Pin Table of Contents Section 1: Context Section 2: Scope 2.1 Problem 2.2 Success Criteria 2.3 Assumptions Section 3: Methodology 3.1 Technical Assumptions 3.2 Data 3.2.1 Data Collection 3.2.2 Data Cleaning and Filtering 3.2.3 Feature Engineering 3.2.4 Data Splitting 3.3 Experimental Design Section 4: Findings 4.1 Results 4.2 Discussions 4.3 Recommendations Section 1: Context Are the university courses students take truly equipping them with the practical skills employers demand? Recent Graduate Employment Survey results highlight this concern, with the proportion of fresh graduates securing employment within six months declining from 94.5% in 2021 to 87.1% in 2024. This trend also affects fresh graduates across various studies. While macro factors such as economic slowdown and the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence (AI) may reduce or reshape entry-level opportunities, industry feedback points to a more actionable issue: skills mismatch . The Ministry of Education (MOE) is responsible for ensuring university education remains relevant to the future of work including digital literacy, core competencies, and technical skills. Therefore, MOE must assess whether current university curricula are aligned with employer skill requirements for “good jobs” (defined as entry-level roles relevant to a graduate’s field of study), and if misalignment exists, identify the specific skills that are not being adequately developed in university. However, MOE currently lacks a granular, data-driven mechanism to assess the alignment between university curricula and the rapidly evolving recruitment landscape. This creates a severe informational blind spot for the Higher Education Planning Office (HEPO) and the Higher Education Policy Division (HEPD). Therefore, this data science team has been tasked with solving this issue. The following sections outline the problem and how our team could help to alleviate it.
